critique: The delivered general result is only a limitation of lower bounds that certify indistinguishability through the displayed two-point TV contraction; it neither rules out nor proves the proposed nuisance-privacy branch. For beta<gamma, the proved lower rate is gamma-smooth while the upper rate is beta-smooth, so the central minimax frontier and even its privacy-free full-class limit remain unresolved. The matching beta=gamma result is a valid full-class causal reduction, but its exponent is inherited directly from private pointwise regression rather than establishing new causal nuisance geometry. The arbitrary-pair interpolation barrier and the honest two-sided bracket constitute a narrow standalone contribution, but not the claimed sharp observational phase frontier.
